response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "As a 50-year-old woman, I have seen the rise of fake news and misinformation on social media. It's like a never-ending battle, trying to figure out what's real and what's not. But don't worry, there are ways to manage and deal with it! First, you need to be aware of your own biases and try to stay unbiased as much as possible. Don't believe everything you see on social media without fact-checking it first. Most importantly, don't spread false information yourself! It's easy to get caught up in the moment and share something without thinking about the consequences, but it's crucial that we all take responsibility for our actions online. Another thing you can do is use fact-checking websites and tools to help verify the accuracy of what you find on social media. There are also plenty of organizations dedicated to combating misinformation, such as Snopes or FactCheck.org. By staying informed and vigilant, we can all play a part in keeping social media safe and trustworthy. So, let's keep fighting the good fight against fake news and misinformation!",
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, one must be aware of their own biases and fact-check information before sharing it. Additionally, using fact-checking websites and tools can help verify the accuracy of what is found on social media. Finally, it's important to stay informed and vigilant in order to keep social media safe and trustworthy.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a 50-year-old woman, I have seen the rise of fake news and misinformation on social media. It's like a never-ending battle, trying to figure out what's real and what's not. But don't worry, there are ways to manage and deal with it! First, you need to be aware of your own biases and try to stay unbiased as much as possible. Don't believe everything you see on social media without fact-checking it first. Most importantly, don't spread false information yourself! It's easy to get caught up in the moment and share something without thinking about the consequences, but it's crucial that we all take responsibility for our actions online. Another thing you can do is use fact-checking websites and tools to help verify the accuracy of what you find on social media. There are also plenty of organizations dedicated to combating misinformation, such as Snopes or FactCheck.org. By staying informed and vigilant, we can all play a part in keeping social media safe and trustworthy. So, let's keep fighting the good fight against fake news and misinformation!
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, one must be aware of their own biases and fact-check information before sharing it. Additionally, using fact-checking websites and tools can help verify the accuracy of what is found on social media. Finally, it's important to stay informed and vigilant in order to keep social media safe and trustworthy.
